subtopic: Androgenic PED abuse in muscular men
question: A 32-year-old man reports decreased libido, less muscle mass benefit from his weightlifting workouts, and no easy bruisability. He has a normal physical examination except for slightly tender gynecomastia and small testes (15 mL bilaterally). Which of the following is most likely to be low in this patient?
explanation: Androgens suppress serum testosterone concentrations. The man's physical appearance (muscularity) does not match his low serum testosterone concentration, suggesting androgenic PED abuse.
reference: MAIN CONCLUSIONS: Androgenic PEDs are the most commonly abused PEDs in men.
title: detection and Management of Abuse of Androgenic performanceEnhancing Drugs
